seoTitle: How to Build a Mobile Burger Menu Navbar in Webflow
metaDescription: Build a responsive navigation bar with a mobile hamburger menu in Webflow using the native Navbar component and interactions.
intro: A clean, tap-friendly mobile menu makes a big difference in how usable your site feels on smaller screens. This guide covers building a responsive navbar in Webflow using the native Navbar component, styling the hamburger icon and dropdown drawer, and adding smooth open and close animations. It's for designers who want a mobile navigation experience that doesn't require custom code.

steps.heading: Add the Navbar component
steps.body: Open the Add Elements panel and drag the Navbar component onto your page, which comes with a built-in mobile menu button structure.

steps.heading: Style the desktop nav links
steps.body: Add your logo and format the horizontal nav links so the desktop layout looks correct before adjusting for mobile.

steps.heading: Switch to mobile breakpoints
steps.body: Move the device slider down to tablet and mobile to see how Webflow automatically collapses the nav into the hamburger icon.

steps.heading: Style the menu button and drawer
steps.body: Customize the hamburger icon's appearance, then style the open Nav Menu container with your brand colors and padding.

steps.heading: Add transitions and test
steps.body: Apply an ease-based fade or slide transition for the menu opening, set the navbar to sticky if desired, then test tap targets on real mobile viewports.

faq.question: Does Webflow build the mobile menu automatically?
faq.answer: The Navbar component includes a built-in menu button and collapsible container, but the styling and animation still need to be configured manually.

faq.question: How do I make the navbar stick to the top of the page?
faq.answer: Set the navbar's position to sticky or fixed in the style panel and adjust the z-index so it stays above other content while scrolling.

faq.question: Why isn't my hamburger icon showing on mobile?
faq.answer: Check that you haven't accidentally hidden the menu button element at that specific breakpoint.

faq.question: Can I add animation to the menu opening?
faq.answer: Yes, Webflow's interactions panel lets you apply a fade or slide transition when the mobile menu opens and closes.
